codeReview.summary: ValidationPipeline implementation is correct. It runs gates sequentially, aggregates messages from all gates, and requires all gates to pass for overall validation to pass. Tests verify all three expected behaviors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No handoff file or worker transcript found in mission directory for worker session 7af9d2f2-5d30-4885-af0a-612e3ec708be. The mission directory at .factory/missions/67c2b690-f32a-4538-8ff8-3eb2790dc44e/ contains features.json but no handoffs/ subdirectory.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ls .factory/missions/67c2b690-f32a-4538-8ff8-3eb2790dc44e/ returns: AGENTS.md, features.json, mission.md, runtime-custom-models.json, state.json, validation-contract.md, validation-state.json - no handoffs/ directory exists

summary: The validation-pipeline feature passes code review. The ValidationPipeline struct correctly implements all three expected behaviors: (1) gates run sequentially via the run() method, (2) messages are aggregated from all gates via extend(), and (3) overall passed=true only when all gates pass (using all_passed &= outcome.passed). The test suite in lib.rs (pipeline_tests module) contains 12 passing tests that verify gate ordering, message aggregation, pass/fail conditions, and context handling. No code issues were found.
